data.title: US-listed SRx Health Solutions has increased its holdings of Bitcoin and Ethereum by a total of $18 million.
data.description: SRx Health Solutions, a U.S.-listed company, has added funds to its digital treasury management strategy to purchase Bitcoin. Following this increase, the company's total cryptocurrency allocation in Bitcoin and Ethereum reaches $18 million. This investment is part of the company's capital allocation strategy, aimed at allocating excess liquidity to assets such as Bitcoin, Ethereum, Opendoor, gold, and silver. SRx Health Solutions stated that it will periodically increase or decrease its holdings based on market conditions, valuations, and other strategic considerations, and that this investment will not affect the company's core business operations.

data.title: Wall Street Firms Embrace Blockchain Technology for Trading
data.description: Annabelle Huang, co-founder of Altius Labs, notes a significant transition among Wall Street trading firms from conventional methods to utilizing on-chain infrastructure. According to NS3.AI, companies such as Jump, Cumberland, and Jane Street are actively engaging in the development of blockchain validator technology, offering market data, and participating extensively in cryptocurrency trading. The rise of stablecoins and tokenized assets is anticipated to enhance liquidity and broaden on-chain market prospects, with future profits likely stemming from improved blockchain utilization.

data.title: WisdomTree expands its tokenized fund onto the Solana blockchain.
data.description: WisdomTree's full suite of tokenized funds has expanded to Solana, further advancing its multi-chain strategy. Institutional and retail investors can now mint, trade, and hold on Solana through the WisdomTree Connect and WisdomTree Prime platforms. Maredith Hannon, Head of Digital Asset Business Development at WisdomTree, stated that this move aims to meet the growing demand for tokenized money market funds, equities, and fixed-income products. Institutional clients can manage their positions directly on Solana, while retail users can purchase tokenized funds via USDC and store them in their self-custodied wallets.

data.title: SRx Health Solutions Invests $18 Million in Bitcoin and Ethereum
data.description: SRx Health Solutions, a company listed on the NYSE American, has announced an investment of $18 million in Bitcoin and Ethereum. According to Foresight News, this move is part of the company's capital allocation strategy. In addition to investing in these cryptocurrencies, SRx Health Solutions plans to allocate excess liquidity into securities and commodities such as gold and silver.

data.title: Bitcoin Market May Be Transitioning to Bearish Structure, Analyst Suggests
data.description:
Bitcoin's market dynamics may be shifting towards a bearish structure rather than experiencing a temporary pullback in a bull market, according to PANews. CryptoQuant analyst Woominkyu highlighted that the supply of Bitcoin in loss (SALL) is on the rise again. Historically, this shift indicates the early stages of a bear market, where losses begin to affect not only short-term holders but also long-term participants.

In previous cycles, such as in 2014, 2018, and 2022, this indicator increased before the actual market bottom was reached, with prices continuing to weaken. The true bottom only formed after a significant expansion in the supply in loss. Currently, the supply in loss remains well below historical capitulation levels, but the directional change itself is significant.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Cap Increases Amid Uncertainty
data.description: The cryptocurrency market capitalization increased by 2.2%, reaching $3.12 trillion, as 90 of the top 100 coins saw gains. According to NS3.AI, Bitcoin's value rose to $89,419, while Ethereum hovered near a crucial breakeven point at approximately $3,020, indicating potential for further movement. Despite temporary ETF outflows and persistent fear in market sentiment, ongoing legislative efforts and macroeconomic trends continue to present both uncertainty and opportunity.

data.title: BlackRock deposited $103 million in BTC and $59.23 million in ETH into Coinbase.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, BlackRock (0x4a2...b82) deposited 1,156.87 BTC (worth $103 million) and 19,644 ETH (worth $59.23 million) into Coinbase, and may continue to deposit more assets.

data.title: Altius Labs co-founder: Wall Street trading firms are moving on-chain.
data.description: Annabelle Huang, co-founder and CEO of Altius Labs, stated that Wall Street's alpha is shifting from physical hosting and nanosecond-level speed advantages to the use of on-chain infrastructure. Jump has leveraged its high-frequency trading experience to develop the high-performance validator client Firedancer for Solana, and its supported project, DoubleZero, is seeking to commercialize its global private fiber optic and submarine cable network to reduce latency. Cumberland is providing real-time crypto market data to Pyth Network. Jane Street traded over $110 billion in cryptocurrencies in 2024 and recently hired the former head of infrastructure architecture at Copper. With stablecoins and the tokenization of real-world assets injecting liquidity, the on-chain market will continue to expand, and future alpha will stem from the production, sorting, and monetization of block space.

data.title: Global Trade Dynamics Shift as Major Agreements Exclude U.S.
data.description: In January 2026, three pivotal trade agreements were finalized, marking a notable shift in global trade dynamics without U.S. involvement. According to NS3.AI, the European Union successfully negotiated significant deals with India and MERCOSUR countries, enhancing its trade relations. Concurrently, Canada and China are working on a separate agreement, although progress is challenged by U.S. tariff threats. U.S. President Donald Trump's trade policies have indirectly influenced these developments, with Washington offering support but not actively participating in the negotiations.

data.title: Cactus Custody Unveils Advanced Self-Custody Platform for Institutions
data.description: Cactus Custody has introduced a new self-custody platform designed for institutional use, utilizing Multi-Party Computation (MPC) technology to bolster digital asset security and mitigate single-point key risks. According to NS3.AI, the platform incorporates compliance tools like Chainalysis and Notabene to adhere to AML and Travel Rule regulations, providing institutions with comprehensive control over their assets. Furthermore, Cactus Custody has integrated Circle's USDC infrastructure to enhance workflow interoperability for its institutional clients.

data.title: Bleap, the project of former Revolut head, has raised $6 million in seed funding.
data.description: Bleap has announced the completion of a $6 million seed funding round, led by Blossom Capital. The project's primary focus is developing an on-chain financial application built around self-custody and stablecoin payments. Founded by former Revolut executives Joao Alves and Guilherme Gomes, the project aims to use blockchain as a settlement layer to provide payment, cross-border transfer, and savings yield functionality. Bleap is currently launched in Europe and Mexico and plans to use the new funding to expand to more regions in Latin America and launch cross-chain transaction capabilities. Furthermore, Bleap plans to reschedule the launch of its native token to early 2027.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 90,000 USDT with a 2.25% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jan 28, 2026, 11:57 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 90,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 90,044.9375 USDT, with a narrowed 2.25% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Pinterest Restructures Workforce Amid AI Focus
data.description: Pinterest has revealed plans to reduce its workforce by nearly 15% as it shifts resources towards artificial intelligence-driven initiatives and restructures its operations. According to NS3.AI, this decision aligns with a broader trend in the tech industry, which is increasingly focusing on AI, sparking concerns about potential workforce displacement. Despite the reduction in staff, Pinterest intends to reinvest in strategic growth areas related to AI. The restructuring is expected to incur costs between $35 million and $45 million.
